channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Santa Augmentcode Intent Ep.2
item.description: The Master Gift List That Writes Itself 🎄 Accompanying source code repository: Santa Augmentcode Intent Every year, on the first of December, I sit at my great oak desk and open the Master Gift...

item.title: RAG Components Explained: The Building Blocks of Modern AI
item.description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) is one of the most powerful techniques for making Large Language Models (LLMs) smarter, more factual, and more up-to-date. Instead of relying only on what an...

item.title: Santa Augmentcode Intent Ep.3
item.description: Santa Plans, Elves Build — Coordinator & Specialist Agents 🧝 Accompanying source code repository: Santa Augmentcode Intent A common misconception about the North Pole is that I build all the toys....

item.description: Every Elf Gets a Workbench — Isolated Workspaces 🔨 Accompanying source code repository: Santa Augmentcode Intent In the early Workshop days, all the Elves shared one big table. It seemed...

item.title: Fixing Production Data in Rails: Lessons from a 6,000-Row Backfill
item.description: Fixing a bug in production is usually straightforward. But what happens when the data in the database itself is broken? That’s when things get tricky. I learned this when a routine deploy quietly...

item.title: From Outbox to Email Delivery: Extending the Kafka Flow in Spring Boot
item.description: In the previous article, I showed how to create an order, save an outbox event in the same transaction, and publish that event to Kafka using the outbox pattern. This new step is the natural...

item.title: DURABILITY
item.description: For this task, I am trying to understand durability. That means once a transaction is committed, the data should not be lost even if the system crashes. First I check the current data in the...

item.title: CLAUDE.md Is the New .env — And Most Developers Treat It Like a README
item.description: Then I actually looked at what Boris Cherny — the guy who built Claude Code — puts in his. And what Trail of Bits runs across their security audit repos. And what the teams winning Anthropic’s...

item.title: Idempotency Situation
item.description: In this task, I understood the concept of idempotency in a wallet system. In real applications like GPay or PhonePe, sometimes the same request may be sent multiple times due to network issues or...

item.title: New Bernie Sanders AI Safety Bill Would Halt Data Center Construction
item.description: The US senator said on Tuesday that a moratorium would give lawmakers time to "ensure that AI is safe." Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez will introduce a similar bill in the House in the weeks ahead.

item.title: The best deals we’ve found from Amazon’s Big Spring Sale (so far)
item.description: Amazon loves to manufacture an event. March is historically a dry spell for deals; however, with Amazon’s third annual Big Spring Sale, which starts today and runs through March 31st, the retail...

item.title: With Sift Stack, two ex-SpaceX engineers are bringing the software that helped launch rockets to the factory floor
item.description: Sift is building the data infrastructure for advanced manufacturing.

item.title: Dell made its new Pro laptops thinner and tweaked their naming scheme, again
item.description: Dell is announcing a new lineup of Pro laptops to go with its recent Precision line relaunch. The new models have rejiggered names: Dell Pro 3, Dell Pro 5, and Dell Pro 7. The Pro 3 and Pro 5...

item.title: Reolink’s first solar-powered floodlight camera offers wire-free home security
item.description: Reolink's new solar-powered floodlight camera is now available. Announced earlier this year at CES, the Solar Floodlight Camera costs $109.99 but is on sale for $87.99 for the launch. Most...

item.title: The Download: reawakening frozen brains, and the AI Hype Index returns
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. This scientist rewarmed and studied pieces of his friend’s...

item.title: Sony and Honda ain’t feelin’ the Afeela anymore
item.description: Sony and Honda have lost that lovin' Afeela. The joint venture announced today that it would be discontinuing the $90,000 Afeela 1 electric sedan, as well as the unnamed Afeela SUV concept, as it...

item.title: Samsung’s Galaxy A57 gets thinner, faster, and more expensive
item.description: Samsung has announced its two newest midrange phones, the Galaxy A57 and A37. Both phones benefit from a jump to IP68 water-resistance and some improved AI features, but the bigger upgrades are...

item.title: The Kill Chain Is Obsolete When Your AI Agent Is the Threat
item.description: In September 2025, Anthropic disclosed that a state-sponsored threat actor used an AI coding agent to execute an autonomous cyber espionage campaign against 30 global targets. The AI handled...

item.title: Russian Hacker Sentenced to 2 Years for TA551 Botnet-Driven Ransomware Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) said a Russian national has been sentenced to two years in prison for managing a botnet that was used to launch ransomware attacks against U.S. companies. Ilya...

item.title: Agentic commerce runs on truth and context
item.description: Imagine telling a digital agent, “Use my points and book a family trip to Italy. Keep it within budget, pick hotels we’ve liked before, and handle the details.” Instead of returning a list of...

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude Code gets ‘safer’ auto mode
item.description: Anthropic has launched an "auto mode" for Claude Code, a new tool that lets AI make permissions-level decisions on users' behalf. The company says the feature offers vibe coders a safer...

item.title: Device Code Phishing Hits 340+ Microsoft 365 Orgs Across Five Countries via OAuth Abuse
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are calling attention to an active device code phishing campaign that's targeting Microsoft 365 identities across more than 340 organizations in the U.S., Canada,...

item.title: Sen. Wyden Warns of Another Section 702 Abuse
item.description: Sen. Ron Wyden is warning us of an abuse of Section 702: Wyden took to the Senate floor to deliver a lengthy speech, ostensibly about the since approved (with support of many Democrats) nomination...

item.title: Lucid Bots raises $20M to keep up with demand for its window-washing drones
item.description: Lucid Bots has seen demand accelerate over the last year for its window cleaning drones and power washing robots.

item.title: It’s always a good time to revisit Super Mario Bros. Wonder
item.description: The best Super Mario games are ones I find myself coming back to again and again. I'm not sure I want to count how many copies of Super Mario Bros. 3 and Super Mario World I own across various...

item.title: Arm’s CEO Insists the Market Needs His New CPU. It Could Piss Everyone Off
item.description: Arm just confirmed the rumors: It’s producing its own chip for the first time. CEO Rene Haas explains why this won’t alienate the many chipmakers who license the company’s designs.

item.title: The AI Hype Index: AI goes to war
item.description: AI is at war. Anthropic and the Pentagon feuded over how to weaponize Anthropic’s AI model Claude; then OpenAI swept the Pentagon off its feet with an “opportunistic and sloppy” deal. Users quit...

item.title: FCC Bans New Foreign-Made Routers Over Supply Chain and Cyber Risk Concerns
item.description: The U.S. Federal Communications Commission (FCC) said on Monday that it was banning the import of new, foreign-made consumer routers, citing "unacceptable" risks to cyber and national security....

item.title: The BB-777 is the ultimate in boombox nostalgia
item.description: Bumpboxx is fully embracing nostalgia with its latest boombox, the BB-777, which is modeled very closely on the legendary Sharp GF-777. A real deal GF-777 will set you back over $2,000 for one in...
